body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 41/2026
Made under: Health Professions And Occupations Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 24, 2026

B.C. Reg. 41/2026, deposited March 24, 2026, under theHEALTH PROFESSIONS AND OCCUPATIONS ACT[sections 25 and 27]. Ministerial Order M120/2026, dated March 21, 2026. I, Josie Osborne, Minister of Health, order that, effective April 1, 2026, (a)	the Health and Care Professionals Regulation, B.C. Reg. 131/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 1, (b)	the Nurses and Midwives Regulation, B.C. Reg. 133/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 2, and (c)	the Regulated Health Practitioners Regulation, B.C. Reg. 129/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 3. — J. OSBORNE,Minister of Health. 1	Section 46 of the Health and Care Professionals Regulation, B.C. Reg. 131/2025, is amended (a)	in subsection (1) (b) by striking out "of the cardiorespiratory and neuromusculoskeletal systems", (b)	by repealing subsection (1) (c) and substituting the following: (c)	advising on the physical health of patients., (c)	in subsection (2) by striking out ", as they relate to the systems referred to in subsection (1) (b)", and (d)	in subsection (2) (a) by striking out "as it relates to the diseases, disorders and conditions referred to in subsection (1) (b)". 1	The Nurses and Midwives Regulation, B.C. Reg. 133/2025, is amended by repealing section 6 (3) (a). (a)	by repealing subsection (1) (b) and substituting the following: (b)	preventing, treating and managing physical and mental diseases, disorders and conditions of midwifery patients and their newborns and infants, including carrying out appropriate emergency measures when necessary;, and (b)	 in subsection (2) by adding "and mental" after "physical". 1	The Regulated Health Practitioners Regulation, B.C. Reg. 129/2025, is amended in item 53 of the table to section 4 of the Schedule of Restricted Activities by striking out "installation" and substituting "instillation". Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC expands scope of practice for health professionals, including midwives and physios
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has amended three health profession regulations to broaden what certain practitioners can do. The changes remove limits on physiotherapists (they can now advise on all physical health, not just specific body systems), allow midwives to treat mental as well as physical conditions in their patients and newborns, and fix a typo in the restricted activities table for regulated health practitioners. These updates took effect on April 1, 2026.
